title: The search for oil
scene: 石油勘探已成为现代工业中最昂贵的赌博之一。地质学家研究岩层、分析地震数据，以标定可能蕴藏石油的区域；一口勘探井耗资数百万美元，须钻至数千米深处。取自油井的岩样在实验室中分析，以判定是否存在可开采的储油层。并非每个前景看好的地点都能产出具有商业价值的原油；大陆架下已发现大型储量，但开采需要先进技术。从初步勘探到全面投产，往往历时数年。尽管风险巨大，重大发现的可能仍推动着全球的石油勘探。本课语法重点是 where 引导的地点定语从句，以及勘探流程中的被动语态与分词定语。

words.eg: Geologists identify areas where oil may have accumulated. 地质学家标定可能蕴藏石油的区域。
Rock samples are analysed to determine whether a viable reservoir exists. 岩样经分析以判定是否存在可开采的储油层。
Seismic data is analysed to reveal underground structures. 地震数据经分析以揭示地下结构。
A single exploratory well can cost millions of dollars. 一口勘探井耗资数百万美元。
Not every site yields commercially viable quantities of crude oil. 并非每个地点都能产出具有商业价值的原油。
Large reserves have been located beneath the continental shelf. 大陆架下已发现大型储量。
Geologists search for commercially viable oil reserves. 地质学家寻找具有商业开采价值的石油储量。
The search for oil has become one of the most expensive gambles in industry. 石油勘探已成为工业中最昂贵的赌博之一。
Wells drilled in promising zones cost millions each. 在前景看好的区域钻探的油井每口耗资数百万。
As the wind freshened there came a sudden squall and the ship made a strange movement, listing to port.
from dust devils and squalls up to continent-size eddies that only satellites can see.
The modern weather models work with a grid of points of the order of sixty miles apart,
Suppose every sensor gives perfectly accurate readings of temperature,
pressure, humidity, and any other quantity a meteorologist would want.
pressure, humidity, and any other quantity a meteorologist would want.
The computer will still be unable to predict whether Princeton, New Jersey, will have sun or rain on a day one month away.
At noon the spaces between the sensors will hide fluctuations that the computer will not know about,
tiny deviations from the average.
Two factors weigh heavily against the effectiveness of scientific research in industry.
In so far as any inquiry is a secret one,
that it is a positive advantage to them not to keep them secret.
The process from initial exploration to full production often takes many years.

grammar.explain: areas where oil may have accumulated 中 where 引导地点定语从句，先行词表地点，从句中作状语。wells drilled、taken from the well 用过去分词作定语表被动。易错点：先行词是地点且从句缺地点状语时用 where，不用 which。
examples: Geologists identify areas where oil may have accumulated over millions of years. （地质学家标定石油可能历经数百万年积聚的区域。）
Rock samples taken from the well are analysed in the laboratory. （取自油井的岩样在实验室中分析。）

grammar.explain: 勘探流程描述多用被动：has been located、is analysed、are drilled。现在完成时被动强调至今结果，一般现在时被动陈述常规流程。易错点：reservoir 是被定位的对象，用 has been located。
examples: A large reservoir has been located beneath the continental shelf. （大陆架下已发现大型储油层。）
Seismic data is analysed to reveal underground structures. （地震数据经分析以揭示地下结构。）

tips: ①先行词表地点、从句缺地点状语用 where；②wells drilled、taken from 是过去分词作定语表被动；③has been located 是现在完成时被动，is analysed 是一般现在时被动。

title: The Butterfly Effect
scene: 混沌理论告诉我们，初始条件的微小变化可能产生截然不同的结果。气象学家早已承认，长期天气预报本质上不可靠；理论上，巴西一只蝴蝶扇动翅膀，或许会在德克萨斯引发龙卷风。这一著名的「蝴蝶效应」揭示了确定性方程与不可预测现实之间的深刻鸿沟。若初始温度略高一点，整个天气模式便会改变；要是科学家更早接受这些局限，本可节省数十亿徒劳的预测开支。科学界认识到复杂系统中的混沌之后，该理论才获得认可。科学能描述自然规律，却无法精确预测一切。本课语法重点是虚拟语气（对现在/过去的假设），以及 Had ...、Only when ... 等倒装结构。

words.eg: Only when scientists recognised the chaos did the theory gain acceptance. 科学界认识到混沌之后，该理论才获认可。
There is a deep gap between deterministic equations and unpredictable reality. 确定性方程与不可预测现实之间存在深刻鸿沟。
Long-range weather forecasts are inherently unreliable. 长期天气预报本质上不可靠。
Meteorologists have long admitted that long-range forecasts are unreliable. 气象学家早已承认长期预报不可靠。
The flap of a butterfly's wings might set off a tornado in theory. 理论上，蝴蝶扇动翅膀或许会引发龙卷风。
A butterfly's wings in Brazil might set off a tornado in Texas. 巴西的蝴蝶翅膀或许会在德克萨斯引发龙卷风。
Reality remains unpredictable despite precise equations. 尽管有精确方程，现实仍不可预测。
Science cannot predict everything with precision. 科学无法精确预测一切。
Meteorologists have long admitted the limits of prediction. 气象学家早已承认预测的局限。
Yet a great many processes depending on such research are sought for with complete secrecy until the stage at which patents can be taken out.
for fear the agents of other firms should be able to trace the kind of research they are likely to be undertaking.
In the organization of industrial life the influence of the factory upon the physiological and mental state of the workers has been completely neglected.
Modern industry is based on the conception of the maximum production at lowest cost,
and without giving any consideration to the effects produced on the individuals and on their descendants by the artificial mode of existence imposed by the factory.
and without giving any consideration to the effects produced on the individuals and on their descendants by the artificial mode of existence imposed by the factory.
and without giving any consideration to the effects produced on the individuals and on their descendants by the artificial mode of existence imposed by the factory.
and without giving any consideration to the effects produced on the individuals and on their descendants by the artificial mode of existence imposed by the factory.
Sculpture demands response to form in three dimensions. 雕塑要求感知三维形态。
The shape and dimensions of the skyscrapers depend entirely on the necessity of obtaining the maximum income per square foot of ground,
and of offering to the tenants offices and apartments that please them.
Civilized men like such a way of living.
While they enjoy the comfort and banal luxury of their dwelling, they do not realize that they are deprived of the necessities of life.
While they enjoy the comfort and banal luxury of their dwelling, they do not realize that they are deprived of the necessities of life.
While they enjoy the comfort and banal luxury of their dwelling, they do not realize that they are deprived of the necessities of life.
The modern city consists of monstrous edifices and of dark, narrow streets full of petrol fumes and toxic gases,

grammar.explain: 对现在/将来不真实：if + 过去式（were），主句 would do；对过去不真实：if + had done，主句 would/could have done。易错点：would 不进 if 从句；与过去相反主句用 would have done。
examples: If the initial conditions were even slightly different, the outcome would be utterly changed. （若初始条件略不相同，结果便会彻底改变。）
If forecasters had possessed perfect data, they still could not have predicted the storm. （即使预报员拥有完美数据，他们仍无法预测风暴。）

grammar.explain: Had + 主语 + done 置句首 = If ... had done，省略 if；Only when ... 置句首，主句用 did + 主语 + 动词原形。易错点：Had the temperature been ... = If the temperature had been ...。
examples: Had the initial temperature been a fraction higher, the entire pattern would have altered. （若初始温度略高一点，整个模式便会改变。）
Only when scientists accepted the limits of prediction did chaos theory gain recognition. （科学界认识到预测局限之后，混沌理论才获认可。）

tips: ①现在假设：if + 过去式（were）+ would do；②过去假设：if + had done + would have done；③Had ... been 置句首可省略 if，Only when ... 后主句倒装。

title: Secrecy in industry
scene: 工业间谍活动与商业秘密的保护，是现代经济中不为人知的一面。大公司投入巨资研发新产品，却须防范对手窃取配方；接触机密信息的员工往往须签署保密协议。秘密难以保守这一事实令各行业高管担忧——含专有数据的文件存放在限制出入的保险库中，一次泄密就可能使公司损失数百万美元。存在因疏忽谈话向竞争对手泄露关键信息的风险。公司以严格的内部规章和持续警惕来保护竞争优势。本课语法重点是 the fact/risk that ... 同位语从句，以及修饰 employees、documents 的定语从句。

words.eg: The fact that industrial espionage is widespread alarms executives. 工业间谍活动普遍存在这一事实令高管担忧。
Documents that contain proprietary data are stored in secure vaults. 含专有数据的文件存放在保险库中。
Employees must sign confidentiality agreements. 员工须签署保密协议。
A single breach of confidentiality can cost millions. 一次泄密就可能损失数百万美元。
The fact that secrets are difficult to keep alarms executives. 秘密难以保守令高管担忧。
Companies must guard against rivals stealing their formulas. 公司须防范对手窃取配方。
Careless talk may leak vital information to rivals. 疏忽谈话可能向竞争对手泄露关键信息。
Proprietary documents are stored in secure vaults. 专有文件存放在安全保险库中。
Companies safeguard their edge with strict rules and constant vigilance. 公司以严格规章和持续警惕保护竞争优势。
The modern city consists of monstrous edifices and of dark, narrow streets full of petrol fumes and toxic gases,
The modern city consists of monstrous edifices and of dark, narrow streets full of petrol fumes and toxic gases,
torn by the noise of the taxicabs, lorries and buses, and thronged ceaselessly by great crowds.
torn by the noise of the taxicabs, lorries and buses, and thronged ceaselessly by great crowds.
It was only in the twentieth century that the metropolis became the dominant form of settlement.
In the early days of the settlement of Australia, enterprising settlers unwisely introduced the European rabbit.
In the early days of the settlement of Australia, enterprising settlers unwisely introduced the European rabbit.
This rabbit had no natural enemies in the Antipodes, so that it multiplied with that promiscuous abandon characteristic of rabbits.
This rabbit had no natural enemies in the Antipodes, so that it multiplied with that promiscuous abandon characteristic of rabbits.
This rabbit had no natural enemies in the Antipodes, so that it multiplied with that promiscuous abandon characteristic of rabbits.
It caused devastation by burrowing and by devouring the herbage which might have maintained millions of sheep and cattle.
It caused devastation by burrowing and by devouring the herbage which might have maintained millions of sheep and cattle.
Scientists discovered that this particular variety of rabbit (and apparently no other animal) was susceptible to a fatal virus disease, myxomatosis.
Scientists discovered that this particular variety of rabbit (and apparently no other animal) was susceptible to a fatal virus disease, myxomatosis.
What factor helped to spread the disease of myxomatosis?

grammar.explain: the fact/risk + that 从句解释具体内容，that 在从句中不作成分：the fact that espionage is widespread；there is a risk that information may be leaked。易错点：risk 后 that 从句说明风险内容，不是定语从句。
examples: The fact that industrial espionage is widespread alarms many executives. （工业间谍活动普遍存在这一事实令许多高管担忧。）
There is a risk that confidential information may be leaked through careless talk. （存在因疏忽谈话泄露机密信息的风险。）

grammar.explain: employees who have access、documents that contain proprietary data 修饰先行词。be bound to do 表「有义务做」。易错点：先行词指人作主语用 who，指物用 that/which。
examples: Employees who have access to secrets must sign confidentiality agreements. （接触机密的员工须签署保密协议。）
Employees who leave the firm are bound not to disclose trade secrets. （离职员工有义务不泄露商业秘密。）

tips: ①the fact/risk 后解释内容用 that 从句；②employees 用 who，documents 用 that 引导定语从句；③be bound to do 表有义务做，to disclose 是不定式。

title: The modern city
scene: 现代城市是人类最伟大的造物，也是最令人头疼的难题。涌入城市中心的数百万人使老化基础设施不堪重负；城市规划者修建地铁与环城路以缓解拥堵，交通堵塞却依然存在。正是大都市的巨大规模使其如此难以有效管理——随着城市蔓延，受污染与过度拥挤影响的区域不断向外扩展。分区法规将工厂与居住区隔开，但执法仍是持续挑战。直到二十世纪，大都市才成为主导聚居形态；现代城市按自身逻辑生长，往往超出任何规划者的蓝图。本课语法重点是非谓语动词（分词、不定式）和 It is ... that 强调句。

words.eg: It is the sheer scale of the metropolis that makes it difficult to manage. 正是大都市的巨大规模使其难以管理。
Millions flocking to cities strain ageing infrastructure. 涌入城市的数百万人使老化基础设施不堪重负。
Planners build ring roads to ease congestion, yet jams persist. 规划者修建环城路缓解拥堵，堵塞却依然存在。
Areas affected by pollution spread outward as cities sprawl. 城市蔓延时，受污染影响的区域不断向外扩展。
Zoning laws separate factories from residential districts. 分区法规将工厂与居住区隔开。
Enforcement of zoning laws remains a constant challenge. 分区法规的执行仍是持续挑战。
Areas affected by pollution and overcrowding spread outward. 受污染与过度拥挤影响的区域不断向外扩展。
Planners build ring roads to divert traffic from the centre. 规划者修建环城路以疏导市中心交通。
It was only in the twentieth century that the metropolis became the dominant form of settlement. 直到二十世纪，大都市才成为主导聚居形态。
By infecting animals and letting them loose in the burrows, local epidemics of this disease could be created.
Millions of cattle were slaughtered to contain an epidemic that devastated British farming.
Later it was found that there was a type of mosquito which acted as the carrier of this disease and passed it on to the rabbits.
Later it was found that there was a type of mosquito which acted as the carrier of this disease and passed it on to the rabbits.
so that the rabbit population was unlikely to be completely exterminated.
Ironically, Europe, which had bequeathed the rabbit as a pest to Australia, acquired this man-made disease as a pestilence.
Ironically, Europe, which had bequeathed the rabbit as a pest to Australia, acquired this man-made disease as a pestilence.
Ironically, Europe, which had bequeathed the rabbit as a pest to Australia, acquired this man-made disease as a pestilence.
Ironically, Europe, which had bequeathed the rabbit as a pest to Australia, acquired this man-made disease as a pestilence.
It did not, however, remain within the confines of this estate.
and it spread to Britain where wild rabbits are regarded as a pest but where domesticated rabbits,
There has long been a superstition among mariners that porpoises will save drowning men by pushing them to the surface,
or protect them from sharks by surrounding them in defensive formation.
or protect them from sharks by surrounding them in defensive formation.
it is probably a mistake to credit dolphins with any motive of life-saving.

grammar.explain: 现在分词 flocking 作定语表主动（涌入城市的人们）；过去分词 affected 表被动（受影响的区域）；to divert 作目的状语（为了疏导交通）。易错点：people 与 flock 是主动关系用 -ing，areas 与 affect 是被动关系用 -ed。
examples: People flocking to cities from the countryside strain existing infrastructure. （从农村涌入城市的人们使现有基础设施不堪重负。）
Planners build ring roads to divert traffic from the city centre. （规划者修建环城路以疏导市中心交通。）

grammar.explain: It is/was + 被强调部分 + that + 其余部分。强调主语用 It is the sheer scale that ...；强调时间状语用 It was only in the twentieth century that ...。易错点：强调句中 that 不换成 which/who（强调人时可用 who）。
examples: It is the sheer scale of the metropolis that makes it so difficult to manage. （正是大都市的巨大规模使其如此难以管理。）
It was only in the twentieth century that the metropolis became the dominant form of settlement. （直到二十世纪，大都市才成为主导聚居形态。）

tips: ①主动关系用 -ing 作定语（flocking），被动用 -ed（affected）；②表目的用 to do（to divert）；③强调句 It is/was ... that ...，去掉后句子仍完整。

title: A man-made disease
scene: 牛海绵状脑病，俗称疯牛病，是一场人为灾难。牛曾被喂食其他动物的加工残骸，朊病毒在牛群中迅速传播；疫情被发现之前，饲料早已被感染物质污染。为控制摧毁英国畜牧业的疫情，数百万头牛被屠宰。食用受感染牛肉的人可能患上致命的脑病——若农民未改变牛的饲料，这场灾难或许根本不会发生。用肉骨粉喂牛的做法此后已被禁止。这场悲剧警示人们：不可为短期利益牺牲安全。本课语法重点是各时态的被动语态，以及对过去不真实情况的虚拟语气。

words.eg: Bovine spongiform encephalopathy is commonly known as mad cow disease. 牛海绵状脑病俗称疯牛病。
Prions spread rapidly through the herds when cattle were fed infected remains. 牛被喂食感染残骸时，朊病毒在牛群中迅速传播。
The feed supply had been contaminated before the outbreak was detected. 疫情被发现前饲料已被污染。
Millions of cattle were slaughtered to contain the epidemic. 为控制疫情，数百万头牛被屠宰。
The epidemic devastated British farming. 这场疫情摧毁了英国畜牧业。
The feed was contaminated long before the outbreak was detected. 疫情爆发前很久饲料就已受污染。
If farmers had not changed the diet, the catastrophe might never have occurred. 若农民未改变饲料，灾难或许根本不会发生。
If only stricter precautions had been imposed at an earlier stage. 要是更早采取更严格的预防措施就好了。
The practice of using meat-and-bone meal has since been banned. 用肉骨粉喂牛的做法此后已被禁止。
On the occasions when they have pushed to shore an unconscious human being they have much more likely done it out of curiosity or for sport,
In 1928 some porpoises were photographed working like beavers to push ashore a waterlogged mattress.
In 1928 some porpoises were photographed working like beavers to push ashore a waterlogged mattress.
In 1928 some porpoises were photographed working like beavers to push ashore a waterlogged mattress.
it may have been because curiosity attracted them and because the scent of a possible meal attracted the sharks.
It is possible that upon such an occasion a battle ensued, with the sharks being driven away or killed.
In an age of intrigue, reliable intelligence was worth more than any army.
They are constantly after the turtles, who peacefully submit to all sorts of indignities.
One young calf especially enjoyed raising a turtle to the surface with his snout,
and then shoving him across the tank like an aquaplane.
and then shoving him across the tank like an aquaplane.
In another game, as the turtle swims across the oceanarium, the first porpoise swoops down from above and butts his shell with his belly.
In another game, as the turtle swims across the oceanarium, the first porpoise swoops down from above and butts his shell with his belly.
In another game, as the turtle swims across the oceanarium, the first porpoise swoops down from above and butts his shell with his belly.
He no sooner recovers his equilibrium than the next porpoises comes along and hits him another crack.
In another game, as the turtle swims across the oceanarium, the first porpoise swoops down from above and butts his shell with his belly.
He no sooner recovers his equilibrium than the next porpoises comes along and hits him another crack.

grammar.explain: were slaughtered 一般过去时被动；had been contaminated 过去完成时被动（污染发生在 was detected 之前）；has been banned 现在完成时被动（since 提示）。易错点：「污染」在「发现」之前，用 had been contaminated。
examples: Millions of cattle were slaughtered to contain the epidemic. （为控制疫情，数百万头牛被屠宰。）
The feed supply had been contaminated with infected material before the outbreak was detected. （疫情被发现前，饲料早已被感染物质污染。）

grammar.explain: 对过去不真实：if + had done，主句 might/would have done；If only + had done 表对过去的遗憾。易错点：might never have occurred 与 had not changed 搭配表与过去事实相反。
examples: If farmers had not changed the cattle's diet, the catastrophe might never have occurred. （若农民未改变牛的饲料，这场灾难或许根本不会发生。）
If only stricter precautions had been imposed at an earlier stage. （要是更早采取更严格的预防措施就好了。）

tips: ①污染发生在发现之前：had been contaminated；②禁止至今有效：has been banned；③与过去相反：if + had done + might/would have done，If only + had done 表遗憾。

title: Porpoises
scene: 鼠海豚是最聪慧的海洋哺乳动物之一，却每年数千头死于渔网误捕。一群鼠海豚通过人耳听不见的声学信号交流；它们靠天然声呐导航，将高频声波从水中障碍物反弹回来。若干物种现已濒危，面临污染与栖息地破坏的威胁——若保护法规更早实施，无数鼠海豚本可获救。要是我早知道误捕危机，就会帮忙倡导更安全的渔网。研究者从未观察到这些生物之间如此复杂的社交行为。唯有通过国际合作，才能确保鼠海豚继续在公海中繁衍生息。本课语法重点是虚拟语气（含 Had I known 倒装），以及 Never before 等否定词引起的部分倒装。

words.eg: Thousands of porpoises die each year as bycatch in fishing nets. 每年数千头鼠海豚死于渔网误捕。
Porpoises are among the victims of bycatch in commercial fishing. 鼠海豚是商业捕捞误捕的受害者之一。
A pod communicates through acoustic signals beyond human hearing. 一群鼠海豚通过人耳听不见的声学信号交流。
Several species are now endangered by pollution and habitat loss. 若干物种现已因污染和栖息地丧失而濒危。
If conservation laws had been enforced earlier, countless porpoises would have been saved. 若保护法规更早实施，无数鼠海豚本可获救。
Porpoises navigate by a natural form of sonar. 鼠海豚靠天然声呐导航。
A pod of porpoises communicates through acoustic signals. 一群鼠海豚通过声学信号交流。
Only through international cooperation can we ensure porpoises continue to thrive. 唯有国际合作才能确保鼠海豚继续繁衍生息。
Porpoises must continue to thrive in the open sea. 鼠海豚须继续在公海中繁衍生息。
Speculations about its nature have been going on for literally thousands of years,
Speculations about its nature have been going on for literally thousands of years,
and one odd finding that makes the problem puzzling is that it looks very much as if sleeping is not simply a matter of giving the body a rest.
The body's tissues are self-repairing and self-restoring to a degree, and function best when more or less continuously active.
This might be a plausible hypothesis were it not for two factors.
This might be a plausible hypothesis were it not for two factors.
First the electroencephalograph (which is simply a device for recording the electrical activity of the brain by attaching electrodes to the scalp)
First the electroencephalograph (which is simply a device for recording the electrical activity of the brain by attaching electrodes to the scalp)
Some years ago an American psychiatrist named William Dement published experiments dealing with the recording of eye-movements during sleep.
He showed that the average individual's sleep cycle is punctuated with peculiar bursts of eye-movements, some drifting and slow, others jerky and rapid.
He showed that the average individual's sleep cycle is punctuated with peculiar bursts of eye-movements, some drifting and slow, others jerky and rapid.
the first group began to show some personality disorders while the others seemed more or less unaffected.
The implications of all this were that it was not the disturbance of sleep that mattered, but the disturbance of dreaming.
Over the periods their saliva, a mild, digestive juice like our own, was converted into a poison that defies analysis even today.
Over the periods their saliva, a mild, digestive juice like our own, was converted into a poison that defies analysis even today.

grammar.explain: 对过去不真实：if + had done，主句 would have done；Had I known earlier = If I had known earlier（虚拟倒装）。易错点：Had + 主语 + done 置句首省略 if，主句仍用 would have done。
examples: If conservation laws had been enforced earlier, countless porpoises would have been saved. （若保护法规更早实施，无数鼠海豚本可获救。）
Had I known earlier about the bycatch crisis, I would have helped campaign for safer nets. （要是我早知道误捕危机，就会帮忙倡导更安全的渔网。）

grammar.explain: Never before 置句首，主句用过去完成时部分倒装（had scientists observed）；Had I known 是虚拟倒装。Only through ... can we ... 也是部分倒装。易错点：Never before 后常用 had + 主语 + 过去分词。
examples: Never before had researchers observed such complex social behaviour among these creatures. （研究者从未观察到这些生物之间如此复杂的社交行为。）
Only through international cooperation can we ensure that porpoises continue to thrive. （唯有通过国际合作，才能确保鼠海豚继续繁衍生息。）

tips: ①与过去相反：if + had done + would have done；②Had I known = If I had known，省略 if 的虚拟倒装；③Never before 置句首用 had + 主语 + 过去分词。

title: The stuff of dreams
scene: 梦究竟是潜意识欲望的宣泄，还是大脑整理记忆的副产品？弗洛伊德持有这一信念：梦是通往潜意识的皇家大道——他认为，被压抑的愿望在睡眠中以象征形式浮现。神经科学提出不同见解，将生动梦境与快速眼动睡眠及脑活动联系起来；越来越多的证据表明，大脑在梦中巩固记忆。梦中出现的意象往往反映清醒时的焦虑，研究梦有助于心理学家理解有意识心灵所隐藏的情感。梦的内容仍是人类经验中最深奥的谜团之一。本课语法重点是 the belief/evidence that ... 同位语从句，以及动名词作主语和宾语的非谓语用法。

words.eg: Are dreams an outlet for subconscious desires? 梦是潜意识欲望的宣泄吗？
Repressed wishes surface in symbolic form during sleep. 被压抑的愿望在睡眠中以象征形式浮现。
Freud called dreams the royal road to the unconscious mind. 弗洛伊德称梦是通往潜意识的皇家大道。
Neuroscience offers a different view of dreaming. 神经科学对梦提出不同见解。
There is growing evidence that the brain consolidates memories while we dream. 越来越多证据表明大脑在梦中巩固记忆。
Images appearing in dreams often symbolize anxieties experienced while awake. 梦中意象往往象征清醒时的焦虑。
Dream images often reflect anxieties experienced during waking hours. 梦中意象往往反映清醒时的焦虑。
Studying dreams helps psychologists understand concealed emotions. 研究梦有助于心理学家理解被隐藏的情感。
The stuff of dreams remains one of the deepest mysteries of human experience. 梦的内容仍是人类经验中最深奥的谜团之一。
Cats seem to defy the law of gravity. 猫似乎能违抗地心引力法则。
Over the periods their saliva, a mild, digestive juice like our own, was converted into a poison that defies analysis even today.
they could have caught and lived on prey without using poison, just as the thousands of non-poisonous snakes still do.
no running fights with large, fierce rats or tussles with grown rabbits--just a bite and no more effort needed.
no running fights with large, fierce rats or tussles with grown rabbits--just a bite and no more effort needed.
In fact, it would be an assistance to all carnivores though it would be a two-edged weapon when they fought each other.
But, of the vertebrates unpredictable Nature selected only snakes (and one lizard).
But, of the vertebrates unpredictable Nature selected only snakes (and one lizard).
One wonders also why Nature, with some snakes concocted poison of such extreme potency.
One wonders also why Nature, with some snakes concocted poison of such extreme potency.
In the conversion of saliva into poison, one might suppose that a fixed process took place. It did not;
as different as arsenic is from strychnine, and having different effects.
as different as arsenic is from strychnine, and having different effects.
The makers of the nerve poison include the mambas and the cobras and their venom is called neurotoxic.
The makers of the nerve poison include the mambas and the cobras and their venom is called neurotoxic.
The makers of the nerve poison include the mambas and the cobras and their venom is called neurotoxic.
The makers of the nerve poison include the mambas and the cobras and their venom is called neurotoxic.

grammar.explain: the belief/evidence + that 从句解释具体内容：Freud held the belief that dreams are the royal road；there is evidence that the brain consolidates memories。that 在从句中不作成分。易错点：evidence 后 that 从句是同位语，说明证据的内容。
examples: Freud held the belief that dreams are the royal road to the unconscious mind. （弗洛伊德持有这一信念：梦是通往潜意识的皇家大道。）
There is growing evidence that the brain consolidates memories while we dream. （越来越多的证据表明，大脑在梦中巩固记忆。）

grammar.explain: 动名词作主语（Studying dreams helps ...）；现在分词作定语表主动（images appearing in dreams）；avoid 后接 doing（avoid jumping to conclusions）。易错点：appearing 表主动「出现的意象」，不是 appeared。
examples: Studying dreams helps psychologists understand repressed emotions. （研究梦有助于心理学家理解被压抑的情感。）
Psychologists avoid jumping to conclusions when interpreting dream symbols. （心理学家在解读梦的象征时避免草率下结论。）

tips: ①the belief/evidence 后解释内容用 that 从句；②动名词作主语：Studying dreams helps ...；③images appearing 是现在分词作定语表主动，avoid 后接 doing。

title: Snake poison
scene: 蛇毒是自然界最复杂的生化武器之一，却也能救人性命。毒液含数十种毒素，有的攻击血液，有的攻击神经系统；毒液影响神经系统的蛇是地球上最危险的物种之一。从毒液中分离出的有用化合物用于心脏病药物——被咬后须迅速注射抗蛇毒血清以中和毒素，取蛇毒需要技巧、勇气与沉稳。针对世界上大多数最致命蛇种已研制出抗蛇毒血清；一剂即可致命之物，经提炼后或可治愈其他药物无法治疗的疾病。本课语法重点是 whose 引导的定语从句和过去分词定语，以及各时态的被动语态。

words.eg: Snake venom contains dozens of different toxins. 蛇毒含数十种不同毒素。
Some toxins attack the blood, others the nervous system. 有些毒素攻击血液，有些攻击神经系统。
Antivenom must be administered quickly after a bite. 被咬后须迅速注射抗蛇毒血清。
Antivenom must be administered without delay. 抗蛇毒血清须立即注射。
Useful compounds isolated from venom are used in heart medication. 从毒液中分离出的化合物用于心脏病药物。
Antivenom is given to neutralise the poison. 注射抗蛇毒血清以中和毒素。
Compounds isolated from venom are used in medicines. 从毒液中分离的化合物用于药物。
Antivenom serum has been developed for most deadly species. 针对大多数致命蛇种已研制抗蛇毒血清。
What kills in one dose may, in refined form, heal serious conditions. 一剂致命之物经提炼后或可治愈重病。
Vipers (adders) and rattlesnakes manufacture the blood poison, which is known as haemolytic.
Vipers (adders) and rattlesnakes manufacture the blood poison, which is known as haemolytic.
Vipers (adders) and rattlesnakes manufacture the blood poison, which is known as haemolytic.
and the effects on these of viperine poison is almost immediate.
From 1914 to 1924 he was supreme and unchallenged.
and devised the protagonist he played in every film he made,
the good-bad man, the accidental, noble outlaw,
or the honest, but framed cowboy, or the sheriff made suspect by vicious gossip;
or the honest, but framed cowboy, or the sheriff made suspect by vicious gossip;
and in both the history and the mythology of the vanished frontier,
and in both the history and the mythology of the vanished frontier,
And although no period or place in American history has been more absurdly romanticized,
myth and reality did join hands in at least one arena,
the conflict between the individual and encroaching civilization.
And Chinese, Korean, Filipino and Indian engineers are graduating in droves from California's colleges.
were bewildered by politicians, bankers and businessmen,

grammar.explain: whose 表所属关系：snakes whose venom attacks the nervous system。过去分词作定语表被动：compounds isolated from venom。易错点：whose 引导定语从句，表示「其毒液……的蛇」。
examples: Snakes whose venom affects the nervous system are among the most dangerous. （毒液影响神经系统的蛇是最危险的物种之一。）
Compounds isolated from venom are used in medicines for heart disease. （从毒液中分离出的化合物用于心脏病药物。）

grammar.explain: must be administered 情态动词被动；are extracted 一般现在时被动；has been developed 现在完成时被动。易错点：antivenom 是被注射、被研制的对象，用被动语态。
examples: Antivenom must be administered quickly after a snake bite. （被蛇咬后须迅速注射抗蛇毒血清。）
Antivenom has been developed for most of the world's deadliest snake species. （针对世界上大多数最致命蛇种已研制出抗蛇毒血清。）

tips: ①whose 表所属：snakes whose venom ...；②isolated from 是过去分词作定语表被动；③must be administered 是情态动词被动，has been developed 是现在完成时被动。

title: William Shakespeare
scene: 威廉·莎士比亚一五六四年生于埃文河畔斯特拉特福，一六一六年在故里去世，享年五十二岁。他留给世界三十七部戏剧和一百五十四首隽永的十四行诗；莎士比亚创作的戏剧仍在全球各地上演。他回到斯特拉特福时，已写完大部分最伟大的悲剧与喜剧，许多作品首演于伦敦环球剧场。他声名鹊起的伊丽莎白时代，是英国文学的黄金时期——作者身份争议虽时而激烈，却丝毫未损其声誉。四个世纪过去，他对人性的洞察依旧鲜活有力。本课语法重点是修饰 plays、era 的定语从句（含介词+which），以及交代先后的过去完成时。

words.eg: By the time he returned to Stratford, he had written most of his greatest tragedies. 回到斯特拉特福时，他已写完大部分最伟大的悲剧。
He had written most of his greatest tragedies and comedies before returning home. 回家前他已写完大部分最伟大的悲剧与喜剧。
He left the world thirty-seven plays and a hundred and fifty-four sonnets. 他留给世界三十七部戏剧和一百五十四首十四行诗。
The Elizabethan era was a golden age of English literature. 伊丽莎白时代是英国文学的黄金时期。
Shakespeare flourished during the Elizabethan era. 莎士比亚在伊丽莎白时代声名鹊起。
The authorship controversy has done little to diminish his reputation. 作者身份争议丝毫未损其声誉。
His insight into human nature remains as fresh as ever. 他对人性的洞察依旧鲜活有力。
He left thirty-seven plays of enduring beauty. 他留下了三十七部隽永的戏剧。
The authorship controversy has done little to diminish his reputation. 作者身份争议丝毫未损其声誉。
and unhorsed by fences, laws and alien taboos.
and unhorsed by fences, laws and alien taboos.
Hart's good-bad man was always an outsider, always one of the disinherited,
We still do; living in a world in which undeclared aggression, war, hypocrisy,
We still do; living in a world in which undeclared aggression, war, hypocrisy,
chicanery, anarchy and impending immolation are part of our daily lives,
chicanery, anarchy and impending immolation are part of our daily lives,
chicanery, anarchy and impending immolation are part of our daily lives,
we all want a code to live by.
Why does the idea of progress loom so large in the modern world?
and is becoming more and more manifest.
Although mankind has undergone no general improvement in intelligence or morality,
What does the 'uniquely rational way' for us to communicate with other intelligent beings in space depend on?
the growth of knowledge followed a kind of compound interest law,
which was greatly enhanced by the invention of printing.
the tempo was suddenly raised.
The trickle became a stream: the stream has now become a torrent.

grammar.explain: plays that Shakespeare wrote 限制性定语从句；the Elizabethan era, during which he flourished 介词+which 引导定语从句（during which = in which era）。易错点：介词+which 中 which 指代先行词，介词由从句动词搭配决定。
examples: The plays that Shakespeare wrote continue to be performed worldwide. （莎士比亚创作的戏剧仍在全球各地上演。）
The Elizabethan era, during which he flourished, was a golden age of English literature. （他声名鹊起的伊丽莎白时代是英国文学的黄金时期。）

grammar.explain: By the time he returned ... he had written 表「过去的过去」；had contributed 发生在 argued 所指的过去之前。易错点：returned 是一般过去时，had written 发生在 returned 之前。
examples: By the time he returned to Stratford, Shakespeare had written most of his major plays. （他回到斯特拉特福时，已写完大部分主要戏剧。）
Critics argued that others had contributed to some works attributed to Shakespeare. （评论家认为其他人参与了部分归于莎士比亚的作品。）

tips: ①plays 用 that 引导定语从句；②during which = in which era，是介词+which 结构；③by the time + 过去时，主句用 had done 表更早的过去。

title: Not Guilty
scene: 一位归国旅客在海关被拦下，携带可疑数量的免税香烟与烈酒；商品远超法定限额，官员指控他企图走私。他坚称包裹是朋友所赠，从未拆开——检方未能证明他明知包裹内为何物。若他知道内容，本会在边境毫不犹豫申报；他丝毫没料到看似无害的礼物里藏有违禁品。陪审团裁定他所有指控不成立，宣告无罪。此案引发更广泛的问题：法律认定有罪究竟需要多大程度的主观故意？本课语法重点是对过去不真实的虚拟语气（含 Had ... 倒装），以及 Little did ... 等否定词引起的部分倒装。

words.eg: A returning traveller was stopped at customs with suspicious goods. 一位归国旅客在海关被拦下，携带可疑物品。
Officers accused him of attempting to smuggle duty-free goods. 官员指控他企图走私免税商品。
Little did he suspect that the gifts concealed contraband. 他丝毫没料到礼物里藏有违禁品。
The prosecution failed to prove that he had known the contents. 检方未能证明他明知内容。
The jury acquitted him, returning a verdict of not guilty. 陪审团宣告他无罪。
The jury found insufficient evidence of intent. 陪审团发现故意方面的证据不足。
Had the defendant known the contents, he would have declared them. 若被告知道内容，本会在边境申报。
The jury returned a verdict of not guilty on all charges. 陪审团裁定所有指控不成立。
Little did he suspect that innocent-looking gifts hid contraband. 他丝毫没料到看似无害的礼物藏有违禁品。
The trickle became a stream: the stream has now become a torrent.
The problem now facing humanity is:
It is now being used indifferently for both.
Could any spectacle, for instance, be more grimly whimsical
Could any spectacle, for instance, be more grimly whimsical
than that of gunners using science to shatter men's bodies
what will happen if this twofold use of knowledge,
is accompanied for many days by the smaller albatross,
sufficient sustenance and progress.
The albatross is the king of the gliders,
the class of fliers which harness the air to their purpose,
Duck, and like them the pigeons, are endowed with steel-like muscles,
and these will ply the short wings with such irresistible power
that they can bore for long distances through an opposing gale
Their humbler followers, such as partridges,
Duck, and like them the pigeons, are endowed with steel-like muscles,

grammar.explain: 对过去不真实：if + had done，主句 would have done；If only + had done 表遗憾。Had the defendant known = If the defendant had known。易错点：would have declared 与 had known 搭配表与过去事实相反。
examples: If he had known the contents, he would have declared them at the border. （若他知道内容，本会在边境申报。）
If only he had opened the gifts before packing them in his suitcase. （要是他装箱前打开礼物就好了。）

grammar.explain: Had the defendant known ... 虚拟倒装；Little did he suspect ... 否定词置句首部分倒装（did + 主语 + 动词原形）。易错点：Little 置句首表「丝毫不」，主句须倒装。
examples: Had the defendant known what was inside, he would never have crossed the border. （若被告知道里面是什么，绝不会过境。）
Little did he suspect that the innocent-looking gifts concealed contraband. （他丝毫没料到看似无害的礼物里藏有违禁品。）

tips: ①与过去相反：if + had done + would have done；②Had the defendant known = If the defendant had known；③Little did he suspect 是否定词置句首的倒装。

title: One man's meat is another man's poison
scene: 各有所爱，各有所恶——口味与偏好永远无法完全统一。羊油布丁是英国传统佳肴，却足以令许多外国游客却步；许多英国食客对辛辣食物深感厌恶，拉丁美洲人却乐此不疲。味觉主要在童年形成，后天养成的口味需多年培养——一种文化喜爱的东西，另一种文化或许完全无法欣赏。许多人偏爱熟悉菜肴而非异域风味，这并无不合理之处；培养对陌生美食的口味需要耐心与接触。尊重食物偏好的差异，或许是跨文化理解中最重要的一课。本课语法重点是 prefer A to B、acquired taste 等词汇辨析，以及 find it + adj. + to do 等非谓语结构。

words.eg: Suet pudding is enough to repel many visitors from abroad. 羊油布丁足以令许多外国游客却步。
Many English diners have a deep aversion to highly spiced food. 许多英国食客对辛辣食物深感厌恶。
Our palate is shaped largely in childhood. 味觉主要在童年形成。
Latin Americans relish highly spiced food. 拉丁美洲人喜爱辛辣食物。
Suet pudding is a traditional British delicacy. 羊油布丁是英国传统佳肴。
Many people prefer familiar dishes to exotic ones. 许多人偏爱熟悉菜肴而非异域风味。
It takes patience to cultivate a taste for unfamiliar cuisine. 培养对陌生美食的口味需要耐心。
There is nothing irrational in preferring familiar food. 偏爱熟悉食物并无不合理之处。
Tastes and preferences can never be fully harmonised. 口味与偏好永远无法完全统一。
have a like power of strong propulsion, but soon tire.
You may pick them up in utter exhaustion,
It was only a slip of the tongue. 那只是一次口误。
that seems to help its passage even when the wind is adverse.
though we no longer take omens from their flight on this side and that;
It is difficult for any of us in moments of intense aesthetic experience
It is difficult for any of us in moments of intense aesthetic experience
that shines down to us from a different realm of existence, different and,
And, though the gleams blind and dazzle, yet do they convey a hint of beauty and serenity
That all great art has this power of suggesting a world beyond is undeniable.
and in passing leaves an indefinable longing and regret.
life a vulgar flare amid the cool radiance of the stars,
life a vulgar flare amid the cool radiance of the stars,
if these intimations of a something behind and beyond
If we glimpse the unutterable, it is unwise to try to utter it,
nor should we seek to invest with significance that which we cannot grasp.
Non-auditory effects of noise

grammar.explain: prefer A to B 固定搭配「较偏爱 A 而非 B」，to 是介词；acquired taste 固定搭配「后天养成的口味」，acquired 是过去分词作形容词。易错点：prefer 后接 to 不是 than；acquired taste 不是 acquiring taste。
examples: Many people prefer spicy food to bland dishes. （许多人较偏爱辛辣食物而非清淡菜肴。）
A taste for strong cheese is an acquired taste. （对浓奶酪的喜好是后天养成的口味。）

grammar.explain: find it + adj. + to do 结构（find it hard to enjoy）；It takes ... to do（It takes years to cultivate）。不定式作真正宾语，it 是形式宾语。易错点：find it hard to do 中 to do 不可省。
examples: Visitors from abroad often find it hard to enjoy suet pudding. （外国游客往往难以欣赏羊油布丁。）
It takes years of exposure to cultivate a taste for unfamiliar cuisine. （培养对陌生美食的口味需要多年接触。）

tips: ①prefer A to B，to 是介词不是 than；②acquired taste = 后天养成的口味；③find it + adj. + to do 和 It takes ... to do 中 to do 不可省略。

title: A skeleton in the cupboard
scene: 几乎每个古老家族都有「家丑」——羞于外扬的秘密。一位专营旧家具的古董商在购买的一只橱柜中有了可怕的发现：里面藏着一具人骨，前任物主世代隐瞒。曾拥有这件家具的家族最怕名誉受损——正是在那只旧橱柜里，古董商揭开了埋藏数十年的丑闻。物主提出不惜代价买回家具，以防事态扩散；藏有骸骨的橱柜五十多年来未曾打开。古董商承诺守口如瓶，明白每个家庭都有宁愿遗忘之事。本课语法重点是 in which 引导的定语从句和 It was ... that 强调句。

words.cn: 可怕的；令人毛骨悚然的
words.eg: The dealer made a grisly discovery in a purchased cupboard. 古董商在购买的一只橱柜中有了可怕的发现。
The family had concealed the truth for generations. 这个家族世代隐瞒真相。
It was inside that old cupboard that the dealer uncovered a scandal. 正是在那只旧橱柜里，古董商揭开了丑闻。
The dealer promised to remain discreet. 古董商承诺守口如瓶。
An antique dealer who specialised in old furniture made the discovery. 一位专营旧家具的古董商有了这一发现。
The family feared damage to their reputation above all else. 这个家族最怕名誉受损。
The skeleton had been concealed for generations. 骸骨被隐瞒了世代之久。
The dealer uncovered a scandal buried for decades. 古董商揭开了埋藏数十年的丑闻。
The cupboard in which the bones had been hidden had not been opened for fifty years. 藏有骸骨的橱柜五十多年来未曾打开。
those who dislike noise will sometimes use most inadequate evidence
to support their pleas for a quieter society.
This is a pity, because noise abatement really is a good cause,
A good cause may be discredited by bad science. 好事可能因拙劣科学而名誉扫地。
One allegation often made is that noise produces mental illness.
with the caption 'She was yet another victim, reduced to a screaming wreck'.
with the caption 'She was yet another victim, reduced to a screaming wreck'.
Now the snag in this sort of anecdote is of course that one cannot distinguish cause and effect.
Now the snag in this sort of anecdote is of course that one cannot distinguish cause and effect.
that her neighbours were combining to slander her and persecute her,
that her neighbours were combining to slander her and persecute her,
if you think what it must be like to share the deck of a ship with several squadrons of jet aircraft,
But neither psychiatric interviews nor objective tests
it must be so small that present methods of psychiatric diagnosis cannot find it.
that noise is less dangerous than, say being brought up in an orphanage
What is the main condition for the preservation of the remains of any living creature?

grammar.explain: in which 引导定语从句修饰 cupboard（in which = where）；furniture that had belonged to a distinguished family 限制性定语从句。易错点：the cupboard in which ... 中介词 in 与 which 连用，不可拆开。
examples: The cupboard in which the skeleton was concealed had not been opened for decades. （藏有骸骨的橱柜数十年来未曾打开。）
The antique dealer bought furniture that had belonged to a distinguished family. （古董商购买了曾属名门望族的家具。）

grammar.explain: It was inside that old cupboard that ... 强调地点状语；It was the fear of scandal that prompted ... 强调主语。去掉 It was ... that 后句子仍完整。易错点：强调地点、原因、主语均用 It is/was ... that 结构。
examples: It was inside an old cupboard that the dealer made the grisly discovery. （正是在一只旧橱柜里，古董商有了可怕的发现。）
It was the fear of scandal that prompted the owner to buy back the furniture. （正是对丑闻的恐惧促使物主买回家具。）

tips: ①in which = where，修饰表地点的 cupboard；②强调地点：It was inside ... that ...；③强调主语：It was the fear of scandal that prompted ...
